description: Small, pluggable .NET building blocks with minimal-to-no dependencies, meant to compose into a platform on top of which application business logic is built. The family is early-stage: the Mapper library (explicit object-to-object and LINQ/EF Core projection mapping) is the first published block, with a translation/localization library (compile-time-extracted UI strings via ICU MessageFormat) publishing next. Further building blocks are in development in this repository.
rules: Mapper requires every destination property to be explicitly mapped, marked optional, or ignored; unmapped properties throw at configuration/build time. There is no convention-based or name-based auto-mapping and no mapping attributes. Define mappings in a MapperContext subclass, modeled on EF Core's DbContext.
Mapper mapping expressions must be EF Core-translatable (no throw expressions, no delegate invocation) so a single definition drives both in-memory mapping and IQueryable/EF Core projection.

Commands: every command type requires an ICommandHandler<TCommand>; IBatchCommandHandler is additive and never replaces the per-command handler. Validation lives on the handler and runs inside the shared pipeline.

Localization strings are authored once at the call site as an ICU MessageFormat default (the source of truth and final fallback); a Roslyn generator extracts them at compile time. Keys are scoped by category following the ILogger<T> model.

These libraries depend only on the BCL plus opt-in Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection / EF Core integration packages. They avoid runtime reflection on the hot path and are AOT/trim-safe where documented.
